AC-01.evidence: Clicked and dragged across 6 cells in one motion — all 6 painted, no gaps. Fast drag tested: no missed cells at normal interaction speed.

AC-02.evidence: Painted a row of 5 cells, switched to eraser, dragged across 3 — those 3 set to null (checkerboard visible). Remaining 2 unchanged.

AC-03.evidence: Painted a 5×5 enclosed region, filled interior. 9 interior cells filled, border cells unchanged. Fill-on-same-color: no change. Cross-boundary: fill stopped at differently-colored cell. 4-directional confirmed by diagonal non-connectivity test.

AC-04.evidence: Clicked #ff4757 pixel. Active color changed to #ff4757. Tool switched to pen. Immediately drew with new color — confirmed by visual match.

AC-05.evidence: Drew 3 strokes. Ctrl+Z ×3 = empty canvas. Ctrl+Y ×3 = all 3 restored in correct order. Drag stroke counted as 1 undo step (not per-cell) — confirmed.

AC-06.evidence: After fix (scale=32): exported PNG opened in Preview.app. Dimensions: 512×512px. Pixels sharp — no blur or antialiasing. Transparent cells show as transparent in PNG. File size ~3KB for sparse canvas.

AC-07.evidence: Both 1× (16×16px display) and 4× (64×64px display) previews updated immediately on every cell paint. Checkerboard visible for transparent cells in both previews.

AC-08.evidence: Set FEATURE_PIXEL_EDITOR=false. Page rendered blank — no canvas, no toolbar, no palette. Reset to true: full editor rendered.

verifier_context.additional_context: Read ISSUE-001 from STATE.json known_issues before testing AC-06. This is scoped state access as permitted. The issue flagged the export scale bug, which I found independently and then confirmed was the same root cause.
